The corner-guided exploration policy aims to predict long-term discrete goals at the corners for navigation

The corner-guided exploration policy aims to predict a long-term discrete goal at one of the four corners of the bounding box of the scene to efficiently drive the agent towards exploring regions where the target object may be located.
The corner goals efficiently drive the agent to perceive the surroundings and explore regions where the target object is possibly settled.
The category-aware identification policy dynamically learns a discrete confidence threshold to identify the semantic predictions for each category.
Both of these policies, including the corner-guided exploration policy and category-aware identification policy, are trained by Reinforcement Learning (RL) in a low-dimensional discrete action space.

The simultaneous two-policy mechanism and discrete action space design dramatically reduce the learning difficulty for 3D-aware ObjectNav and achieve better performance compared to existing modular-based navigation strategies.
Through extensive evaluation on public benchmarks, the method performs online 3D-aware ObjectNav at 15 FPS while achieving state-of-the-art performance in navigation efficiency.
The method outperforms all other modular-based methods in both efficiency and success rate with up to 30x times less computational cost.
The main contributions of the work include presenting the first 3D-aware framework for the Object-Nav task.
The main contributions also involve building an online point-based construction and fusion algorithm for efficient and comprehensive understanding of floor-level 3D scene representation.

Our framework simultaneously performs two complementary policies, the exploration policy and identification policy.
The exploration policy predicts a long-term discrete corner goal ge to drive the agent to explore the surrounding environment.
The identification policy evaluates the 3D points M3D at each step and outputs a target object goal gf if its semantic prediction is confident and consistent.
The gf will be set as the approaching target for the agent once it exists, otherwise the agent will navigate to the long-term corner goal ge.

An online point-based construction algorithm is extended to organize the 3D points and empower semantic fusion and consistency estimation for the 3D scene representation.
The design is tailored for a comprehensive scene understanding of the ObjectNav agent.
The 3D scene representation is represented as point clouds at time step t.
At time step t, the agent can obtain the point position P (t) by back-projecting all the depth images into the 3D world space via their corresponding poses.
The agent can obtain the point position P (t) by back-projecting all the depth images into the 3D world space via their corresponding poses.
The agent can obtain the point position P (t) by back-projecting all the depth images into the 3D world space via their corresponding poses.
The construction algorithm dynamically allocates occupied 3D blocks {Bk} along with their index k maintained by a tree-based method.
Each block Bk is defined by the boundary of constant length (10cm) along the X, Y and Z axes.
The points pl,x ∈ [Xmin(Bk), Xmax(Bk)] be recorded by the block Bk.
Given any 3D point pi, the algorithm can achieve efficient neighborhood retrieval with the corresponding block index k.
Furthermore, a one-level octree Oi for each point pi is constructed to obtain the fine-grained spatial information among points.
Specifically, we connect each point with its nearest points in the eight quadrants of the Cartesian coordinate system.
Powered by this point-based construction strategy, give any point, we can efficiently querying this point with its neighbor points by blocks retrieval and octree.
This algorithm for organizing 3D points can run at 15 FPS while requiring reasonable memory resources (about ∼ 500 MB for one entire scene).

Analysis of Computational Cost. Our framework is extremely memory efficient, which requires about 0.5GB for one scene.
Our framework is extremely memory efficient, which requires about 0.5GB for one scene, and can perform online construction and semantic fusion at a frame rate of 15 FPS.
Moreover, our method requires only 48 GPU hours to train a 3D-aware agent on MP3D dataset to achieve the SOTA performance.
This is significantly faster (30x) than other existing reinforcement learning based methods, and is comparable to supervised learning modular-based methods.
In this work, we present a 3D-aware framework for object goal navigation.
The method is based on a 3D point-based construction algorithm to observe the 3D scenes.
